old_value: {"id":"cluster-api-provider-oci-joekr-22y.8","title":"NLB multi-backend-set membership fails with backend name collision","description":"When OCICluster configures multiple API server backend sets on NLB, CAPOCI attempts to add the same control-plane backend name to each backend set. OCI NLB returns HTTP 409 Conflict (Mismatching request) for the secondary backend set, leaving it with zero backends.","design":"Update machine backend reconciliation for NLB to use deterministic backend names scoped by backend set (while preserving single-backend behavior). Ensure membership checks and delete flow use the same derived naming so reconcile remains idempotent and cleanup-safe.","acceptance_criteria":"1) For NLB with multiple API-server backend sets, control-plane machines are added to every desired backend set without OCI 409 conflicts. 2) Existing single-backend-set behavior remains unchanged. 3) Delete flow removes set-scoped backends from all desired/actual backend sets safely. 4) Unit tests cover create/delete name derivation and multi-backend membership idempotency.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"joe.kratzat@oracle.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:41:31Z","created_by":"Joe Kratzat","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:41:31Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented NLB backend membership naming fix: backend names are now deterministic per backend set for multi-backend NLB, preserving legacy naming for single/default backend set. Added legacy-name fallback checks for idempotent create/delete migration safety. Updated unit tests in cloud/scope/machine_backendsets_test.go."}

old_value: {"id":"cluster-api-provider-oci-joekr-22y.9","title":"Add regression test for default+secondary NLB backend set naming","description":"Add a focused unit regression test for multi-backend NLB with backend sets apiserver-lb-backendset and apiserver-lb-backendset-2 to ensure backend membership reconcile uses legacy name for default set and deterministic derived name for secondary set.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Test covers create membership with backend sets [apiserver-lb-backendset, apiserver-lb-backendset-2]. 2) Expected CreateBackend request for default set uses backend name machine.Name. 3) Expected CreateBackend request for secondary set uses machine.Name + stable hash suffix. 4) Test passes in cloud/scope package.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"joe.kratzat@oracle.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:48:50Z","created_by":"Joe Kratzat","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:48:50Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented focused regression test TestNLBCreateMembership_DefaultAndSecondaryBackendSetNaming covering backend sets apiserver-lb-backendset + apiserver-lb-backendset-2. Test asserts legacy backend name for default set and deterministic hash-suffixed name for secondary set. Validation: go test ./cloud/scope -run ... and go test ./cloud/scope"}
old_value: {"id":"cluster-api-provider-oci-joekr-22y.9","title":"Add regression test for default+secondary NLB backend set naming","description":"Add a focused unit regression test for multi-backend NLB with backend sets apiserver-lb-backendset and apiserver-lb-backendset-2 to ensure backend membership reconcile uses legacy name for default set and deterministic derived name for secondary set.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Test covers create membership with backend sets [apiserver-lb-backendset, apiserver-lb-backendset-2]. 2) Expected CreateBackend request for default set uses backend name machine.Name. 3) Expected CreateBackend request for secondary set uses machine.Name + stable hash suffix. 4) Test passes in cloud/scope package.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"joe.kratzat@oracle.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:48:50Z","created_by":"Joe Kratzat","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:48:59Z"}

new_value: Implemented focused regression test and validated in cloud/scope

old_value: {"id":"cluster-api-provider-oci-joekr-22y.10","title":"Use per-backend-set backend port for API server LB membership","description":"Secondary API-server backend sets currently register control-plane backends on port 6443 unconditionally, which can cause OCI NLB 409 Mismatching request and prevents secondary backend set population. Reconcile backend membership should use backend-set-specific listenerPort when configured.","design":"In machine LB/NLB membership reconcile, derive target backend port from canonical backend set definition: listenerPort if set, else API server default port. Preserve single-backend behavior. Update create/delete logic and focused tests for default+secondary backend sets.","acceptance_criteria":"1) For multi-backend-set NLB with secondary listenerPort, CreateBackend uses that backend-set port. 2) Default backend set continues to use 6443 unless overridden. 3) Existing single-backend behavior remains unchanged. 4) Unit tests cover per-backend-set port selection for create flows.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"joe.kratzat@oracle.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:23:16Z","created_by":"Joe Kratzat","updated_at":"2026-03-05T16:23:16Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented per-backend-set backend port selection in machine backend membership reconciliation. LB and NLB create flows now register backends on listenerPort when configured, else default control-plane endpoint port. Added LB delete fallback for desired vs legacy backend naming. Added focused regression test asserting default set uses 6443 and secondary set uses 9345 for apiserver-lb-backendset-2. Validation: go test ./cloud/scope"}
